reasoning: Imperatives match the bare root, so the prefix is empty.
All 2sg forms begin with o- (often surfacing as w- before a vowel).
All 3sg forms are built from a- plus the root.
All 3pl forms show ba- before the root.
When a- or ba- attaches to an a-initial root, the two a’s contract (e.g., a+asa → asa).
One a is deleted in the aa sequence.
Intervocalic b disappears (e.g., a+bina → aina).
The deletion applies when b is between two vowels.
In 2sg, o- changes before vowels.
o becomes w before a following vowel (e.g., o+isa → wisa).
The o→w change occurs before a vowel.
Apply aa-contraction first so it affects forms like a+asa but not forms where aa is created later.
Apply o→w next; doing this before deleting b prevents obV sequences from turning into wV.
Delete intervocalic b last to yield outputs like oota and aakisa.
o- + bakisa → obakisa; delete intervocalic b → oakisa.
a- + bakisa → abakisa; delete intervocalic b → aakisa.
ba- + bakisa → babakisa; delete intervocalic b → baakisa.
Imperative is the bare root.
a- + anda → aanda; aa-contracts → anda.
ba- + anda → baanda; aa-contracts → banda.
o- + solola; no rule applies before consonant.
a- + solola; no further changes.
o- + ponama; no change before consonant.
ba- + ponama; no further change.
Imperative is the bare root.
a- + bowa → abowa; delete intervocalic b → aowa.
ba- + bowa → babowa; delete intervocalic b → baowa.
Imperative is the bare root.
o- + balusa → obalusa; delete intervocalic b → oalusa.
ba- + balusa → babalusa; delete intervocalic b → baalusa.
o- + longa; no further rule applies.
a- + longa; unchanged.
ba- + longa; unchanged.
